focus: Risk + edge cases: surface adoption risks per finding across sync triad, hook contracts, advisor scoring, validate.sh, and spec-folder migration; identify reject-with-rationale candidates.

graphEvents.label: Reject static ledger as routing authority

label: Managed-block policy is useful as ownership metadata but unsafe as broad installer authority over AGENTS.md
risk: SPAR prepends or replaces a generated block around instruction files, but internal instruction files carry hard gates and runtime precedence; broad installer ownership could erase or reorder human-governed policy.

label: Two-axis command collapse is unsafe because internal suffixes mix execution modes, feature flags, lifecycle, and executor config
risk: A mode x scope matrix hides that :with-research and :with-phases are feature flags while :auto/:confirm are execution modes and deep loops also expose executor selection.

label: Static tools ledger must not become routing authority over Skill Advisor or MCP schemas
risk: Advisor routing depends on live freshness, fail-open behavior, prompt-safe diagnostics, lane attribution, and 0.8/0.35 confidence/uncertainty thresholds; a seed-once ledger can drift.

label: Template manifest work needs migration gates because the existing corpus is validator-sensitive
risk: Current workspace counts show 736 spec.md files and 478 numbered spec-like directories; strict validation, phase-parent detection, metadata requirements, and legacy packets make consumer artifact deletion unsafe.

label: Runtime target manifests should follow ownership semantics instead of leading installer work
risk: Path placement alone does not encode hook behavior, command-owned YAML workflows, MCP discovery, or runtime-specific precedence.

label: Generated SPAR-inspired artifacts must not become authoritative over live advisor, MCP, validator, or instruction-file contracts unless a packet explicitly changes those contracts with tests.
SPAR managed block installer has warning-only malformed marker handling and a single marker namespace.
Internal suffix surface separates execution modes from feature flags and executor configuration.
Current workspace contains 736 spec.md files and 478 numbered spec-like directories, so migration should be sampled and dry-run before template manifest changes.

direction: Broad installer-owned AGENTS.md rewrites using SPAR managed_block semantics
reason: Internal instruction files are governance surfaces with hard gates and runtime precedence; broad generated ownership would create sync and deletion risk.

direction: Static tools.yaml-style ledger as capability or routing source of truth
reason: Internal advisor and MCP surfaces are live, scored, fail-open, and thresholded; a seed-once ledger is suitable only as diagnostic inventory.

direction: Two-template consumer-facing compression of system-spec-kit artifacts
reason: Validators and resume/memory workflows require multiple consumer artifacts and metadata across existing packets; compression belongs behind source composition only.
